applyTo: "**"
---
# Audit & Change Log Standard

*Normative guidance for the project's **durable, committed, history-as-knowledge** record: an append-only **audit log** of every meaningful prompt, skill, and script, and a curated **change log** of every meaningful design decision. It governs how every skill records what it did, how the four design-shaping skills capture decisions, how both feed the knowledge graph, and how a human or a fresh agent session reads the project's own past. The Knowledge Visualization Standard (`knowledge-visualization.md`) governs the artifact graph; this document governs the **activity and decision history** that runs alongside it. The viewer and the `/auditlog` CLI render it; the script bundle (`scripts/audit-log.py`) is the only tool that writes it.*

Normative keywords (**MUST**, **SHOULD**, **MAY**, **MUST NOT**) follow RFC 2119.

The governing idea: **a coding session's reasoning is the most valuable thing it produces and the first thing lost when the session ends.** An agent's session store is ephemeral and private to that session; the next Copilot or Claude Code session starts blind to it. So the project keeps its *own* committed memory of what was prompted, what was done, and what was decided — in plain append-only files that travel with the repo. The audit log answers *"what has happened here?"*; the change log answers *"what was decided, and why?"*. Together they make work **compound across sessions** instead of evaporating.

---

## 0. The two logs (one bundle)

| File | What it is | Written by | Shape |
|---|---|---|---|
| `docs/audit/audit-log.jsonl` | **Audit log** — every meaningful prompt / skill run / script / action | every skill (Audit Mandate, §2) + the `/auditlog` skill | append-only JSONL |
| `docs/audit/change-log.jsonl` | **Change log** — every meaningful design decision / change | the four design-shaping skills + discerned changes (Change Mandate, §3) | append-only JSONL |
| `docs/audit/audit-data.js` | **Derived** `window.AUDIT_DATA` for the viewer (loadable over `file://`) | `audit-log.py` regenerates it on every write | derived — never hand-edited |
| `docs/audit/index.html` | the **interactive viewer** (timeline · search · toggle audit/changes) | self-bootstrapped from `templates/audit-explorer.template.html` | regenerated; never hand-edited |
| `docs/audit/audit-log.md` | the **knowledge-graph hub node** (frontmatter; §4) | the skill that first creates the bundle | a graph artifact (`type: doc`) |

**AL0 — JSONL is the source of truth; the JS is derived.** The two `*.jsonl` files are the canonical record — append-only, one JSON object per line, so each session adds lines and git diffs stay clean (the same pattern as `docs/health-history.jsonl`). `audit-data.js` and `index.html` are **derived projections** regenerated by `audit-log.py`; where they disagree with the JSONL, the JSONL wins and is re-rendered. Nothing hand-edits the derived files.

**AL0.1 — All writes go through the script bundle.** The audit and change logs are written **only** via `docs/ai-forward-pack/scripts/audit-log.py` (`append`, `change`, `import`), never by hand-appending JSON or generating an ad-hoc script at prompt time — exactly as graph mechanics go only through `docs-graph.py` (V18). The script assigns the id, stamps the time, escapes the content for the viewer, and re-renders in one step.

---

## 1. The audit-log entry schema

Each audit entry records one meaningful action. The five **required** fields are the user-facing minimum; the rest are the enrichment that makes the log searchable, graph-linked, and useful to a future session.

```json
{
  "id": "al-0007",                       // assigned by the script (al-NNNN), the redo/reference key
  "shortname": "design-gateway",         // REQUIRED — a short human label
  "datetime": "2026-06-27T14:28:09Z",    // REQUIRED — ISO-8601 UTC (auto if omitted)
  "session": "2dbe541d-…",               // REQUIRED — the session id (links to session history)
  "prompt": "Run /design on the payment gateway…",  // REQUIRED — the prompt / command, verbatim
  "summary": "Produced docs/design/payment-gateway.md; 3 failure modes dispositioned.", // REQUIRED — what the result was
  "kind": "skill",                       // skill|command|script|prompt|commit|manual|session-import
  "skill": "design",                     // the skill that ran, if any
  "tool": "Copilot CLI",                 // the agent/tool that ran it
  "actor": "@handle",                    // who initiated it (optional)
  "artifacts": ["docs/design/payment-gateway.md"], // files created/changed — graph links (§4)
  "tags": ["payments", "resilience"],    // keywords for search
  "outcome": "success",                  // success|partial|failed|blocked
  "change": "cl-0003",                   // link to a change-log entry, when this action was also a decision
  "git": { "sha": "…", "short": "…", "branch": "main", "pushed": true }  // optional, via --git
}
```

**AL1 — The five required fields.** Every audit entry **MUST** carry `shortname`, `datetime`, `session`, `prompt`, and `summary`. The `prompt` is recorded **verbatim** (so it can be copied and re-run); the `summary` states **what the result actually was** (the artifact produced, the outcome — not a restatement of the prompt). The script refuses an entry missing any required field rather than inventing one (D2 — no guessing).

**AL2 — The enrichment fields, and why they were added.** Beyond the five, an entry **SHOULD** carry the fields that make the log *operational* rather than a flat diary. Each earns its place:
- **`id`** — a stable `al-NNNN` so an entry can be referenced and **re-run** (the `/auditlog` redo).
- **`kind`** + **`skill`** + **`tool`** — so the timeline can be filtered by *what kind of thing happened*, *which skill*, and *which assistant* — the difference between a searchable record and a wall of text.
- **`artifacts`** — the files the action touched, which are **graph links** (§4): the audit log joins to the knowledge graph through them, so "what produced this design?" is answerable.
- **`tags`** + **`outcome`** — keyword search and the honest record of whether the action succeeded, was partial, failed, or was blocked (a failed/blocked action is *more* worth recording, not less).
- **`change`** — when an action was also a meaningful decision, it links to the change-log entry (§3), so the two logs cross-reference.
- **`git`** — the commit context at the time, for actions worth pinning to a sha.

**AL3 — Granularity: meaningful, not every keystroke.** An audit entry is written for a **meaningful unit of work** — a skill run, a script invocation, a deliberate prompt that changed the repo or the plan — not for every tool call or trivial read. The bar is *"would a future session want to know this happened?"*. Over-logging buries the signal; under-logging loses the history. When in doubt for a load-bearing action, log it.

**AL4 — No secrets or PII (a hard line).** The audit and change logs are **committed to the repo**, so an entry **MUST NOT** contain secrets, credentials, tokens, or personal data (Responsible-AI Policy; Observability O11). Reference `@handles`, not emails. Record *that* a credential was rotated, never the credential. Run `scrub.py` over the logs if untrusted content may have entered them; redact a `prompt`/`summary` before logging rather than after.

---

## 2. The Audit Mandate — every skill writes to the log

**AL5 — Append an audit entry as the last action.** Every skill, as part of its **last action** (alongside the Discoverability Mandate, V10), **MUST** append an audit-log entry recording the run:

```bash
python3 docs/ai-forward-pack/scripts/audit-log.py append \
  --shortname "<skill>-<subject>" --session "<id>" --skill <skill> --kind skill \
  --tool "<assistant>" --prompt "<the user prompt, verbatim>" \
  --summary "<what the run produced>" --artifact docs/<...> --tag <keyword> [--git]
```

This is the activity-history sibling of writing frontmatter + syncing the index: a skill run that left no trace in the audit log is, like an un-indexed artifact, **not done**. For a long prompt, use `--prompt-file -` (stdin) or pass the whole entry with `--from-json -`. The `/auditlog` skill itself is a **reader** and does not log its own browsing (AL3).

**AL6 — Scripts and notable manual actions log too.** A deployable script with a lasting effect (a migration run, a scrub, a generated artifact) and a deliberate human/agent action that changed the repo **SHOULD** append an entry with the appropriate `kind` (`script` / `manual` / `commit`). The point is a single timeline a future session can read, regardless of *what* did the work.

---

## 3. The Change Mandate — capturing meaningful decisions

The change log is the **curated** subset: not every action, but every action that **changed the design or a decision**. It is to the audit log what an ADR is to a commit — the *why* that outlives the *what*.

```json
{
  "id": "cl-0003",
  "datetime": "2026-06-27T14:40:00Z",
  "session": "2dbe541d-…",
  "kind": "architecture",                // architecture|design|knowledge|migration|decision|spec|other
  "skill": "define-architecture",
  "title": "Adopt the Cascade archetype for ingest",
  "prompt": "Design the ingest pipeline for 10M events/day…",
  "summary": "Chose LOA Cascade (A) over a single-tier classifier; tiers T0→T2→T3.",
  "rationale": "95% of events resolve deterministically; reserves the frontier tier for the residual.",
  "artifacts": ["docs/architecture.md", "docs/adr/0004-ingest-archetype.md"],
  "supersedes": "cl-0001",               // optional — a prior decision this revises
  "audit_ref": "al-0019",                // optional — the audit entry for the same action
  "git": { "before": "840c7c5", "after": "1b2c3d4", "branch": "main",
           "pushed": true, "commits": ["1b2c3d4 feat: ingest cascade"] }
}
```

**CL1 — The four design-shaping skills capture a change by default.** `/collectknowledge`, `/define-architecture`, `/design`, and `/migrate` **MUST**, when they produce or alter a load-bearing decision, append a change-log entry capturing the **prompt** that drove it and a **summary** of the result, via:

```bash
python3 docs/ai-forward-pack/scripts/audit-log.py change \
  --title "<the decision>" --kind <architecture|design|knowledge|migration> --skill <skill> \
  --session "<id>" --prompt "<driving prompt>" --summary "<the decision/result>" \
  --rationale "<why, one line>" --artifact docs/<...> --git-before "<sha captured at grounding>"
```

These four are singled out because their entire purpose is to *make or change a design decision* — the knowledge most expensive to lose. Other skills (`/specify`, `/investigate`, `/adopt`) **SHOULD** add a change entry when they cross the same bar (a settled spec, a root cause that reshapes the design, an adopted architecture); `/implement` does so when a decision was made mid-build rather than merely executed.

**CL2 — Capture git before and after.** A change entry **MUST** record the git context **around** the decision: the `before` sha (captured at the skill's **grounding** step, passed as `--git-before`) and the `after` sha + the **commits and push status** at write time (captured automatically). This is what lets a reader see *which commits realized this decision and whether they shipped* — the decision tied to the code that enacted it. When git is unavailable, the fields are null and the entry is still valid.

**CL3 — Discerning *other* meaningful changes.** Beyond the four skills' explicit capture, a meaningful change is **discerned** from the repo's own signals, so decisions made outside a skill run are not lost. The heuristics (advisory — `audit-log.py suggest` surfaces them for a human/agent to promote):
- a **new ADR** (`docs/adr/`) or **decision note** (`docs/notes/`, V17) not yet referenced by any change entry — a decision was recorded;
- a **commit** since the last change entry whose message signals a decision (`feat`, `BREAKING`, `migrate`, `arch`, `decision`, `adr`);
- a material change that triggered **`review-suggested` propagation** (V16) — by definition a contract/requirement/decision/shape changed;
- a change to `docs/architecture.md`, a new/changed `docs/design/**`, or a schema/migration artifact.
A discerned change is **promoted** to the change log with `audit-log.py change` (linking the artifact and the commits), not left implicit. `suggest` never writes — it advises.

---

## 4. Knowledge-graph & session-history integration

**AL7 — The audit bundle is a graph node.** The bundle is registered in the knowledge graph through a hub artifact `docs/audit/audit-log.md` carrying the V2 frontmatter record (`id: audit-log`, `type: doc`, `owner`, a real summary, `review-by`) and at least one typed link (`relates-to` / `documents`) into the graph, so the Docs Explorer surfaces the project's history alongside its artifacts and it is never an orphan (V10). The `.jsonl`/`.js`/`.html` files are **data**, not graph nodes (the scan indexes `.md` only); the hub `.md` is the node that represents them.

**AL8 — Entries link to artifacts (the join).** An entry's `artifacts` are repo-relative paths that, where they are graph artifacts, **are** graph links — so the activity log and the artifact graph are one connected knowledge base: from a design node you can find the run that produced it, and from a run you can reach the artifact and its neighbors. Grounding (V15) may consult the audit log for *recent activity on the artifacts in scope* as part of loading prior context.

**AL9 — The audit log is the durable counterpart to the ephemeral session store.** A coding assistant's session store (its turn/event history) is **per-session, private, and not committed**; a different or future session cannot read it. The audit log is the **committed, durable, shared** projection of that activity, keyed by `session`, so the history **compounds**: every new session reads `docs/audit/` (and the change log) at grounding and inherits what prior sessions prompted, did, and decided. Where a session export is available, `audit-log.py import --file <export.json>` ingests its turns into the audit log (mapping `prompt`/`summary`/`session`/`datetime`), bridging the ephemeral store into the durable record — but the primary mechanism is each skill appending as it goes (AL5), so the log stays current without any export step.

**AL10 — Read the history at grounding.** A skill's grounding step (its first action) **SHOULD**, alongside the V15 graph traversal, glance at the recent audit/change history for the artifacts in scope (`audit-log.py list` / `search --keyword`) so prior decisions and prior attempts inform the work — the activity-history half of "ground in what the repo already knows" (Rigor Protocol Stage 0).

---

## 5. The viewer and the CLI

**AL11 — The viewer is a timeline with search and a toggle.** `docs/audit/index.html` (from `templates/audit-explorer.template.html`) renders the bundle: a **default reverse-chronological timeline** grouped by day with a short description per action, each row **expandable** to the full prompt (with a **copy-prompt** button), summary, artifacts, tags, outcome, and — for changes — the git before/after and commits. The top bar **searches by keyword, session, and date range**, and a **toggle switches between Full history (audit) and Changes (the meaningful-change timeline)**. It loads only `audit-data.js`, works over `file://`, and degrades to a plain table with no CDN. It is self-bootstrapped: any `append`/`change`/`render` instantiates it from the template if missing.

**AL12 — The `/auditlog` CLI skill.** `/auditlog` is the command-line lens over the bundle: **list** the last *n* entries (`audit-log.py list --n`), **search** by session/date/keyword (`search`), **get** one entry to **re-run** (`get --id <al-NNNN> --field prompt` emits the prompt to copy or pipe), toggle the **change** view (`--kind change`), open the viewer, and **suggest** unlogged meaningful changes (`suggest`). It is a reader/dispatcher over `audit-log.py`; it adds no behavior of its own and does not log its own browsing.

**AL13 — The prompt-reuse lens (`/prompts`, `/searchprompts`) over the same store.** Prompt *reuse* is a second lens over the **one** store, not a second store: `/prompts` and `/searchprompts` (engine `scripts/prompt-log.py`) browse and search the audit log's prompts as an arrow-navigable stack (↑/↓ move · → expand · ← collapse · Enter reuse) and copy a chosen prompt to the clipboard for paste-and-edit. They read `docs/audit/audit-log.jsonl` (adapting each entry's `prompt`→reusable text), so *every* recorded prompt — skill runs, scripts, and prompts you log — is reusable; and `prompt-log.py add` writes a **`kind: prompt`** audit entry **through `audit-log.py`** (AL0.1, the single writer), so a logged prompt is both reusable here and visible in `/auditlog`. There is **no separate prompt store**; a legacy `<repo>/.aiforward/prompts.jsonl` is read only via an explicit `--store`. `/auditlog` is the broad timeline/search/change-log/viewer lens; `/prompts`+`/searchprompts` are the fast reuse lens — same committed store underneath.

---

## 6. Self-verification checklist

- [ ] Every skill run appended an audit entry with the five required fields + `kind`/`skill`/`tool`/`artifacts` (AL1–AL2, AL5).
- [ ] The entry was written via `audit-log.py` (not hand-appended), and `audit-data.js`/`index.html` were re-rendered (AL0.1, AL11).
- [ ] No secret or PII entered either log (AL4).
- [ ] The four design-shaping skills appended a change entry with `prompt`, `summary`, `rationale`, and git before/after (CL1–CL2).
- [ ] Other meaningful changes were discerned and promoted, not left implicit (CL3).
- [ ] The bundle is a graph node (`docs/audit/audit-log.md` with frontmatter + a typed link); entries link artifacts (AL7–AL8).
- [ ] The history was read at grounding; a session export, if available, was imported (AL9–AL10).

## 7. References

- **`knowledge-visualization.md`** (V1–V18) — the artifact knowledge graph this composes with; V10 Discoverability Mandate (the model for the Audit Mandate), V13 freshness, V16 propagation (a change signal), V17 decision notes (the in-graph cousin of a change entry).
- **`project-memory-and-obsidian.md`** (M1–M9) — the committed-Markdown project memory; the audit/change logs are its append-only activity/decision ledger, JSON-shaped for the viewer.
- **`responsible-ai-policy.md`** + **`observability-and-instrumentation.md`** (O11) + **`scripts/scrub.py`** — the no-secrets/no-PII enforcement for committed logs (AL4).
- **`scripts/audit-log.py`** — the only writer/reader of record; **`templates/audit-explorer.template.html`** — the viewer; **`/auditlog`** — the CLI skill.
- **Session store** — the ephemeral per-session turn/event history the audit log durably and shareably mirrors (AL9).
